vol_description: Volume contains videos redundant with "Adult Degu Repeat Reunion"

This study examines how adult degus establish memories for new individuals. Each video represents one "reunion" session in which a degu is exposed either to a cagemate, a new stranger, or a familiar but recently-acquainted conspecific of the same sex.  Videos are purely natural behavior within dyads; no behavioral or physiological/pharmacological manipulations are performed. Reunions take place approximately every 48 hours, which includes 24 hours of co-habitation with a cagemate followed by a 24 hours separation period.

The videos are named as such: monthday_degu1ID_degu2ID_condition. Specific information about the session, condition, and degus involved can be found in the AdultDeguRepeatReunion_ALL and Degu_AgeWeight spreadsheets.

Study examines how adolescent degus establish memories for new individuals. Each video represents one "reunion" session in which a degu is exposed either to a cagemate, a new stranger, or a familiar but recently-acquainted conspecific of the same sex. Videos are purely natural behavior within dyads; no behavioral or physiological/pharmacological manipulations are performed. Reunions take place approximately each 48 hours, which includes 24 hours of co-habitation with a cagemate followed by a 24 hours separation period.
These videos are of male and female juvenile degus. The methods mirror those used for the adult degus.
Data on play activity during reunions following either separation from a specific peer, or isolation from all peers (or neither).
